arts.ttl: Mark Robins' plan to find answer as 'devastated' Stoke City striker ruled out again
arts.sum: The 29-year-old took a whack on the head in a challenge moments before he suffered this latest injury and new manager Robins is left exploring the mechanics of his movement to try to get to the bottom of the situation and keep him fit. “It’s terrible (for Gallagher) because I think Sam can be anything he wants but it’s a circumstance and situation when he’s picked up an injury and I have to look at that and work out how I can use him in the future without exposing him in an explosive game to too much explosive activity.

arts.ttl: Transfer to Sunderland could cost director his job – Seen as symbol of his input
arts.sum: Sunderland have already sealed a January move for AS Roma midfielder Enzo Le Fee. The French midfielder will arrive at the Stadium of Light on an initial loan deal with an obligation to buy if the Black Cats are promoted to the Premier League. arts.ttl: Narcis Pelach sends message as he moves on after Stoke City sacking
arts.sum: Pelach, who previously worked at Huddersfield Town and Norwich City, has now joined up with new West Ham boss Potter at the London Stadium. Narcis Pelach is 'really grateful' for the opportunity afforded to him after he followed Graham Potter into West Ham United and joined the Irons' new-look coaching unit.

arts.ttl: Mark Robins on the unique challenge at Stoke City
arts.sum: Mark Robins, walking into a new club for the first time in eight years, is weighing up the similarities and differences of the challenge he is taking on at Stoke City. “So every club is different but there’s a way you go about things and what people want to watch is winning football.

arts.sum: Sunderland will pay all of Enzo Le Fée’s wages during his loan spell at the club and be obligated to buy him permanently if they are promoted to the Premier League. That’s according to journalist Eleanora Trotta, who has revealed ‘all the figures’ of the midfielder’s move to Sunderland.
